title: pyrus vernae
intro: Pyrus vernae, also known as the wild cherry or gean, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its showy white flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus vernae include reducing fever and relieving coughs and sore throats, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the flowers, leaves, or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
